entries.title: Promoted to senior engineering manager at Vanta
entries.blurb: Took on a second team. Spending more time on hiring loops, less on PRs — and somehow more on the Figma file.

entries.title: First sourdough loaf I would actually serve to a guest
entries.blurb: Year three of the bread project. Open crumb, blistered crust, no gym-mat aftertaste. Recipe lives in my notes app.

entries.title: Shipped the college football scorigami visualization
entries.blurb: A heatmap of every final score that has ever happened in CFB. Wrote it up for the blog. People emailed.

entries.title: Finished the Leadville Trail 100 MTB
entries.blurb: Twelve hours, two bonks, one rebuilt rear derailleur. Would do it again. Probably.

entries.title: Rebuilt this site in Astro
entries.blurb: Migrated off the old stack. Wanted something tiny, fast, and fun to fork experiments into.

entries.title: Joined Vanta as an engineering manager
entries.blurb: Building teams that experiment, ship, and have fun doing it. Compliance is more interesting than it sounds.

entries.title: Moved to Colorado
entries.blurb: Closer to mountains, farther from humidity. The cycling-to-rest-day ratio has shifted.

entries.title: Wrapped up at Elation Health
entries.blurb: Three years driving product strategy and scaling the platform team. Left with a real fondness for the EHR space.

entries.title: Built the elevation-first bike route planner
entries.blurb: A tiny tool for planning rides by climb instead of distance. Still my most-used personal project.

entries.title: Bought a gravel bike, became insufferable about it
entries.blurb: The hobby that ate every weekend morning. No regrets.

entries.title: Completed the Purdue cybersecurity program
entries.blurb: Twelve weeks of threat modeling, incident response, and accidentally locking myself out of my own lab VMs.

entries.title: Started taking on freelance projects on the side
entries.blurb: Financial dashboards, an early LLM-powered writing assistant, a couple of internal tools. Mostly fun, occasionally instructive.

entries.title: Joined Elation Health
entries.blurb: Came in to help with a strategic pivot. Stayed for the team. Learned that healthcare software is mostly a people problem.

entries.title: Picked up ITIL Foundation
entries.blurb: A management cert with surprisingly useful vocabulary. Learned to say "service catalog" with a straight face.

entries.title: Left Automox
entries.blurb: Two years scaling the engineering org from a dozen to north of fifty. Shipped a lot, slept less.

entries.title: Earned Certified Scrum Professional
entries.blurb: After enough retrospectives, the certification mostly confirmed what the team had already taught me.

entries.title: Joined Automox as an early engineering hire
entries.blurb: Endpoint management, a tiny team, a lot of greenfield. The "do it yourself" era of my career.

entries.title: Left Eli Lilly
entries.blurb: Three years inside a pharma giant taught me how slow systems move and where to push.

entries.title: Started at Eli Lilly
entries.blurb: First job out of school. Internal tools, regulated data, fluorescent lighting.

entries.title: BS in Computer Science, Miami University
entries.blurb: Four years of late nights in Benton Hall. Met most of my closest friends here.

entries.title: Started at Miami University
entries.blurb: Showed up undeclared. Took an intro to programming on a whim and never really stopped.

entries.title: Built my first website on a hand-me-down laptop
entries.blurb: Notepad, a pirated copy of Photoshop, and a Geocities account. Looked terrible. Felt like magic.
